key_insight: Data-efficient fine-tuning of a foundation MACE interatomic potential can recover the barocaloric phase transformation of ammonium sulfate using only 5–10 DFT structures, whereas training from scratch or using the base foundation model is insufficient.
engineering_relevance: This shows a practical path to cheaper, faster atomistic screening of barocaloric materials for solid-state cooling, reducing the DFT data burden needed to model phase transitions accurately.
method: Trained MACE MLIPs with three strategies: from scratch, naive replay fine-tuning, and multihead replay fine-tuning of MACE-MPA-0.
Evaluated each model in molecular dynamics simulations over multiple training-set sizes to test whether the temperature-driven structural phase transition is reproduced.
Compared performance at both standard DFT and hybrid-DFT levels, including the role of dispersion corrections.
evidence_signal: Fine-tuned models reproduced the ammonium sulfate phase transformation with as few as 5 to 10 60-atom DFT configurations, while the foundation model failed and scratch-trained models degraded for small datasets.
watch_item: The approach is system-dependent and still requires careful inclusion of dispersion corrections; robustness outside the fine-tuning domain may differ between replay strategies.
paper_title: Barocaloric phase transformation from data efficient fine-tuning of machine learned interatomic potentials
